title: The neural mechanisms of mindfulness-based pain relief: a functional magnetic resonance imaging-based review and primer.

assets.abstract: The advent of neuroimaging methodologies, such as functional magnetic resonance imaging (fMRI), has significantly advanced our understanding of the neurophysiological processes supporting a wide spectrum of mind-body approaches to treat pain. A promising self-regulatory practice, mindfulness meditation, reliably alleviates experimentally induced and clinical pain. Yet, the neural mechanisms supporting mindfulness-based pain relief remain poorly characterized. The present review delineates eviden

phase_mapping.bridge_rationale: Emptiness — self-dissolution, DMN decoupling, ego dissolution, nondual awareness.
